source_proposition: The authors restrict the Cantonese double-object construction to bei2 'give'; other dative verbs use a theme-before-bei2-recipient pattern.
variety_register_period: Modern Hong Kong Cantonese; author judgments
limitations: Lexical restriction is the authors' analysis, not a corpus frequency result.

source_proposition: A bei2 double-bei/prepositional form is marked but is reported possible when the theme is heavy or focused.
surface_example_or_data_basis: bei2-theme-bei2-recipient with heavy or focused theme
variety_register_period: Modern Hong Kong Cantonese; author judgments
limitations: Markedness varies by context and speaker; no rate is supplied.

source_proposition: The paper separates pure caused-possession, transfer-of-possession, and caused-motion dative verb classes.
surface_example_or_data_basis: Verb inventories include give/issue/provide; bring/lend/sell/give; send/transmit/mail/throw/kick
variety_register_period: Modern Hong Kong Cantonese; theoretical classification
limitations: Class membership and translations are source-attributed theory.

source_proposition: Possession verbs are rejected with a purely spatial heoi3 goal, while caused-motion verbs such as send or throw allow it.
surface_example_or_data_basis: Verb + theme + heoi3 + spatial goal contrasts
variety_register_period: Modern Hong Kong Cantonese; author judgments
limitations: Judgment contrast does not establish categorical community usage.

source_proposition: Three surface patterns are distinguished: V-theme-recipient, V-recipient-theme, and V-theme-bei2-recipient.
surface_example_or_data_basis: Three order templates stated by the authors
variety_register_period: Modern Hong Kong Cantonese; theoretical classification
limitations: Inventory is scoped to the cited analysis.

source_proposition: The V-recipient-theme pattern is described for a limited communication/message verb set.
surface_example_or_data_basis: Verbs glossed inquire, teach, test, request, ask
variety_register_period: Modern Hong Kong Cantonese; author judgments
limitations: Not an exhaustive corpus inventory.

source_proposition: The theme-before-bei2-recipient construction is analyzed as allowing inalienable, alienable, control, and focus possession readings depending on the verb.
variety_register_period: Modern Hong Kong Cantonese; author judgments
limitations: Semantic labels are theory-dependent.

source_proposition: The bei2 double-object construction is judged to license alienable/focus concrete possession and abstract possession, but not the paper's inalienable/control readings.
variety_register_period: Modern Hong Kong Cantonese; author judgments
limitations: Do not convert constructional semantics into parser ontology without independent validation.

source_proposition: The authors reject a simple derivation of the double-object form by deleting the dative marker and instead propose independent polysemous constructions.
surface_example_or_data_basis: Meaning contrasts against the deletion account
variety_register_period: Modern Hong Kong Cantonese; formal analysis
limitations: Theoretical conclusion; not observed distribution.

source_proposition: The chapter treats V-direct-object-indirect-object as an 'inverted' Cantonese double-object order and contrasts it with V-indirect-object-direct-object and an overt-marker form.
limitations: Page-level proposition locator could not be recovered from the image-like PDF in this pass.

source_proposition: The inverted order is reported to depend on a Goal-like indirect object and on lexical/thematic properties of the dative verb.
surface_example_or_data_basis: Order and Goal-role contrasts summarized in the chapter
limitations: Exact example page remains an access boundary; speaker variation is acknowledged.

source_proposition: Prosodic weight, discourse completeness, and speaker variation are presented as relevant to the acceptability of the overt-marker and inverted forms.
limitations: Not a quantified production result; exact page-level extraction remains open.

source_proposition: The chapter advances a null/overt preposition analysis for the relation between inverted and marker-bearing orders.
limitations: Theory is disputed by later constructional accounts.

source_proposition: The authors describe V-direct-object-indirect-object as predominant and V-indirect-object-direct-object as an alternative under emphasis or lexical idiosyncrasy.
limitations: Predominance is not backed by a contemporary corpus sample in this paper.

source_proposition: A V-theme-bei2-recipient oblique form is attested, while preposing bei2-recipient before the main verb is rejected in the cited example.
surface_example_or_data_basis: V DO bei IO versus bei IO V DO
limitations: Single-paper judgments; not a population rate.

source_proposition: Verb classes differ: some allow only V-recipient-theme, some only V-theme-recipient, and some both.
limitations: Inventory is not shown to be exhaustive.

source_proposition: A Source-role indirect object blocks V-theme-recipient in the authors' analysis.
surface_example_or_data_basis: Ambiguous ze3 borrow/lend: V-recipient-theme permits source/goal; V-theme-recipient only Goal/lend-to
limitations: Role labels and judgments remain source-attributed.

source_proposition: A light indirect object can follow the direct object, but a heavy indirect object is difficult there without an overt preposition.
limitations: No production frequencies; weight is not a binary parser rule.

source_proposition: The authors say overt bei2 marks a Goal rather than a Source and that marker deletion becomes difficult when either object is long.
surface_example_or_data_basis: V DO bei IO and length contrasts
limitations: The claimed category and deletion process are contested.

source_proposition: The paper favors treating bei2 as a preposition in this construction rather than a full verb.
surface_example_or_data_basis: Object omission, topicalization, and reduplication diagnostics
limitations: Authors note that further study is needed.

source_proposition: Diachronic evidence is used to argue that V-theme-recipient patterns with V-theme-preposition-recipient rather than deriving from V-recipient-theme.
surface_example_or_data_basis: Ancient-stage order comparisons and oracle-bone counts
limitations: Historical evidence does not establish present-day conversational distribution.

source_proposition: The presentation calls V-theme-recipient the canonical double-object order and says the licensing verb set is very limited.
limitations: Presentation wording is not a lexical census.

source_proposition: Heavy themes are shown with multiple alternatives: canonical order, V-theme-bei2-recipient, zoeng construction, right dislocation, topicalization, and extraposition.
limitations: Inventory is task-oriented, not natural-conversation prevalence.

source_proposition: Prior elicited production counts favor the canonical order but show increased alternatives with more complex themes.
surface_example_or_data_basis: Canonical 238/206/169; V T bei R 13/34/51; zoeng 0/1/15; other 1/11/17 across three theme conditions
limitations: Slide labels the values as frequency percent although they behave as counts; preserve as reported counts with this warning.

source_proposition: The processing experiment used 18 L1 Cantonese participants, 12 item sets, and six construction-by-theme conditions.
surface_example_or_data_basis: DOC versus BA by three theme complexities
limitations: Small elicited experiment.

source_proposition: Accuracy declined with theme complexity, especially for the double-object condition; both construction and complexity effects were reported significant.
limitations: Processing performance is not grammatical acceptability or conversational frequency.

source_proposition: Imitation errors included construction switches, double-bei2 forms, topicalization, repair/restart, and missing arguments.
limitations: Error tokens do not independently license a productive construction.

source_proposition: The author interprets alternatives as potential processing aids for heavy themes and notes that the category of zoeng remains unresolved.
limitations: Interpretive discussion, not direct distribution evidence.

source_proposition: The abstract proposes a historical/dialectal origin for the inverted Cantonese double-object pattern.
limitations: No full evidence, methods, or examples were recovered; cannot carry a distribution claim.

source_proposition: The study classifies 807 bei/俾 occurrences from two 1990s Hong Kong spoken corpora.
surface_example_or_data_basis: HKCanCor about 230k words plus HKCAC about 100k words; orthographic variants combined
limitations: Counts depend on the author's coding; corpora are not contemporary.

source_proposition: Four transfer/dative patterns account for 110, 2, 21, and 61 tokens respectively.
surface_example_or_data_basis: V NP2 bei NP3 110; V-bei NP3 NP2 2; bei NP3 NP2 21; bei NP2 NP3 61
limitations: Percentages are shares of all 807 bei tokens, not conditional rates among dative tokens.

source_proposition: In these corpora, bei-theme-recipient is more frequent than bei-recipient-theme.
limitations: Coding-specific and corpus-period-specific; not a universal preference.

source_proposition: The heavy-direct-object subset is too small for a robust ordering conclusion.
surface_example_or_data_basis: Eight long-object tokens: 3 recipient-theme versus 5 theme-recipient
limitations: Explicitly sparse and inconclusive.

source_proposition: The corpus taxonomy includes omitted-recipient, omitted-theme, both-objects-omitted, fronted-theme, and zoeng variants.
limitations: Functional recoverability and coding criteria are source-specific.

source_proposition: Eight tokens classified as conditional bei(+zoek) NP are a non-transfer homograph/function boundary.
limitations: Must be excluded from recipient-role induction.

source_proposition: The most frequent class in this all-bei taxonomy is a serial/benefactive-like NP1 V NP2 bei NP3 V pattern.
limitations: Broad all-function denominator; final verb may be indispensable because the first verb is not dative.

source_proposition: Causative and passive classes share the surface frame bei NP VP and are distinguished contextually.
limitations: Same spelling and frame do not imply a recipient relation.

source_proposition: The later paper retracts the earlier no-agent passive bei VP pattern because an Excel encoding blank had hidden an intervening noun.
limitations: This correction overrides the earlier taxonomy for that pattern.

source_proposition: The paper reports 13 classes and flags multiple sparse classes as requiring further evidence.
limitations: Taxonomy is an analyst classification, not a parser-ready ontology.

source_proposition: The earlier article supplies a corpus-driven structural taxonomy of Cantonese bei constructions in contrast with Mandarin gei.
limitations: Do not double-count as independent evidence; later classifications control where inconsistent.

exact_locator: earlier no-agent passive class; corrected by Wong 2023/2024 pp. 103-104
source_proposition: The earlier claimed bei VP no-agent passive pattern is not supported after correction of an encoding artifact.
surface_example_or_data_basis: Hidden noun recovered in the source cell
limitations: Retained only as a provenance/correction record, not as positive evidence.

source_proposition: The production study retained 30 participants aged 21-26 and used picture description with bei, zoeng, and sung written primes.
surface_example_or_data_basis: 31 recruited; one excluded; 16 target drawings and 48 prime versions
variety_register_period: Recent Hong Kong Cantonese; elicited online production
limitations: Three participants were fully proficient rather than self-described native; online task is not conversation.

source_proposition: Among 219 selected canonical or double-bei responses, canonical counts were 72/73/8/2/1/0 and double-bei counts 12/34/3/5/3/1 across distance bins 2-7.
variety_register_period: Recent Hong Kong Cantonese; elicited production
limitations: Other constructions were excluded; long-distance cells are very sparse.

source_proposition: Theme-recipient distance did not significantly predict double-bei production in the reported model.
variety_register_period: Recent Hong Kong Cantonese; elicited production
limitations: Null result does not prove no effect.

source_proposition: A descriptive increase in double-bei with distance was not statistically established, and evidence above four syllables came from only 23 tokens from nine participants.
variety_register_period: Recent Hong Kong Cantonese; elicited production
limitations: Authors characterize the evidence as initial/inconclusive.

source_proposition: The unrestricted response task elicited many constructions that were excluded from the canonical-versus-double-bei analysis.
variety_register_period: Recent Hong Kong Cantonese; elicited production
limitations: Selected-response denominator cannot estimate overall construction prevalence.

source_proposition: The dissertation contrasts unmarked double-object V NP NP with V-theme-bei-recipient and claims verbs are lexically associated with one rather than freely alternating.
surface_example_or_data_basis: DOC and SVC templates plus verb inventories
independence_scope: same family as SCREEN-CP021B-011; partial author overlap SCREEN-CP021B-012
limitations: Lexical lists and nonalternation are author analysis, not corpus closure.

source_proposition: For lexical bei2 GIVE the stated order is theme-recipient, while verbs such as teach and owe are described with recipient-theme.
limitations: Not an independent population sample.

source_proposition: The dissertation reports that nominal status and animacy do not generally reverse lexical order; theme pronominalization with bei2 is difficult because of a competing human interpretation, and two pronouns are rejected.
limitations: Judgment-based and theory-linked.

source_proposition: With a heavy theme, bei2 allows canonical theme-recipient, shifted recipient-theme, or a double-bei form in the author's judgments.
limitations: Speaker choice is acknowledged; no rate is supplied.

source_proposition: The double-bei form is judged unavailable with a short theme.
limitations: Conflicts with graded or marked judgments elsewhere; do not make categorical without reconciliation.

source_proposition: A heavy recipient remains phrase-final for bei2, optionally with a second bei2, while heavy-theme order is more flexible.
limitations: Judgment inventory, not production frequency.

source_proposition: When both objects are heavy, the author reports theme-recipient, recipient-theme, and double-bei options for lexical bei2, while other double-object verbs remain recipient-theme.
limitations: Potential speaker variation and formal-theory context.

exact_locator: publisher abstract and metadata; dissertation family locators SCREEN-CP021B-010
source_proposition: The peer-reviewed article covers the anomalous syntax of GIVE within the same research program as the dissertation.
limitations: No independent proposition is counted beyond the accessible dissertation evidence.

source_proposition: The paper distinguishes a single-predicate double-object construction with contiguous objects from a two-predicate serial-verb construction.
limitations: Structural diagnosis is theory-bound.

source_proposition: Some speakers judge both constructions to express transfer/benefactive meanings, with the serial form potentially suggesting effort, insistence, or a non-ultimate source.
limitations: Nuance is explicitly speaker-variable and unquantified.

source_proposition: The bei2 double-object example is theme-before-beneficiary; recipient-theme order is shown for other lexical verbs such as lend and teach.
limitations: Small lexical inventory.

source_proposition: The authors claim the theme in the bei2 double-object form resists pronominalization while the beneficiary may be pronominal, and both non-agent arguments cannot both be pronouns.
limitations: Judgments are analysis-specific and should not become hard constraints without replication.

source_proposition: The typical serial pattern is V-theme-bei2-recipient, and the paper analyzes the verbs as a complex predicate using subject, polarity, and connector diagnostics.
surface_example_or_data_basis: lo2/buy + theme + bei2 + recipient; negation diagnostics
limitations: Formal analysis competes with prepositional accounts.

source_proposition: With ambiguous ze3 borrow/lend, the serial V-theme-bei2-recipient form is reported to force a lend-to reading.
limitations: Single analysis/judgment family.

source_proposition: The transfer/double-object use is illustrated as NP1 bei2 NP2 NP3 with theme before recipient.
variety_register_period: Modern Hong Kong Cantonese; grammaticalization study
limitations: Example is not a frequency statement.

source_proposition: Recipient-before-theme is reported for some Hong Kong speakers when the theme is exceptionally long, with increasing use attributed in cited earlier work to Mandarin contact.
variety_register_period: Modern Hong Kong Cantonese; reported judgment/sociolinguistic citation
limitations: Contact explanation is not independently extracted here.

source_proposition: The paper distinguishes transfer V[+GIVE]-theme-bei2-recipient from beneficiary V[-GIVE]-theme-bei2-beneficiary using verb valency and optionality.
variety_register_period: Modern Hong Kong Cantonese; grammaticalization study
limitations: Valency/category labels are analytical; surface order alone is insufficient.

source_proposition: The recipient phrase is presented as required for a complete transfer example, yet marker omission is also reported especially when the main verb is bei2 and the theme is short.
limitations: Internal tension means no categorical obligatoriness rule is warranted.

source_proposition: Causative/permissive and passive uses share NP1 bei2 NP2 VP; context can make a sentence causative, passive, or ambiguous.
variety_register_period: Modern Hong Kong Cantonese; grammaticalization study
limitations: These are non-recipient boundaries despite the shared spelling/frame.

source_proposition: The permissive/causative bei2 can take aspect in the cited example, supporting the author's verbal analysis.
variety_register_period: Modern Hong Kong Cantonese; author judgment
limitations: Category inference is theory-specific.

source_proposition: In the passive analysis, NP2 is agent and NP1 patient; adverse meaning is not obligatory.
surface_example_or_data_basis: Passive examples including a nonadverse 'praised by boss' judgment
variety_register_period: Modern Hong Kong Cantonese; author judgment
limitations: Non-target boundary only.

source_proposition: An instrument use with inanimate post-bei2 NP is reported from one older Hong Kong speaker, described as nonproductive among young speakers except a fixed expression.
surface_example_or_data_basis: bag-wrap-chicken; water-wash-chicken; bei2 sam gei fixed expression
variety_register_period: Modern/historical Hong Kong and Taishan boundary
limitations: Single-speaker evidence and variety/age boundary; not a productive modern-HK rule.

source_proposition: The author proposes grammaticalization paths from GIVE to beneficiary/IO marker and from GIVE through causative to instrument/passive.
limitations: Historical theory is not parser ontology.

source_proposition: The study is corpus-based and examines the input to Cantonese-English bilingual children for the [bei2-theme-recipient] target.
limitations: No full adult coding table was available; not unrestricted adult conversation.

source_proposition: The abstract reports that the mapping between word order and thematic roles is not consistent in the Cantonese input.
limitations: No exact distribution can be extracted from the abstract.

source_proposition: The study compares productions of 86 parents and 53 three-year-old children and includes full/non-full and VRT/VTR variants.
limitations: Use only abstract-visible claims pending full coding tables.

source_proposition: Adults are reported to use prepositional/serial forms more than ditransitive forms.
limitations: No exact rate or unrestricted-adult register inference is warranted.

exact_locator: paper abstract and examples; six-consultant study
source_proposition: Causative and passive bei2 share NP1 bei2 NP2 VP and some sentences permit both readings.
limitations: Non-target boundary; not transfer/dative distribution evidence.

source_proposition: The preserved exhaustive candidate inventory contains 476 rows, of which 445 are lexical bij2 hits selected for source-level review.
surface_example_or_data_basis: HKCanCor query artifacts summarized in the clean snapshot
independence_scope: independent corpus, but candidate selection only
limitations: Lexical hits are not grammatical analyses; current snapshot preserves ledgers rather than rendered utterance JSON.

source_proposition: HKCanCor is retained as candidate-level evidence for manual proposition testing, not as automatic proof of recipient roles or productive structure.
limitations: Requires direct utterance recovery and manual review before any construction count can support synthesis.

source_proposition: The HKCanCor description supplies provenance for the conversational corpus used by later corpus studies and local source queries.
independence_scope: corpus provenance, not a separate usage sample
limitations: Does not independently analyze neutral bei2 constructions.

exact_locator: paper sections registered in source screen
source_proposition: Historical changes in Cantonese double-object marking are retained only as a diachronic boundary for modern claims.
limitations: Must not be used as contemporary conversational distribution evidence.

source_proposition: The dictionary corroborates that bij2 has multiple lexical/grammatical functions, including give-related and non-transfer uses.
limitations: No grammatical proposition or distribution count is promoted from this source.
